research_gaps.description: No validated naturalistic classroom-based eye-tracking protocol exists for ADHD assessment. Existing eye-tracking paradigms are conducted in laboratory or clinical settings, which may not capture real-world attentional dynamics and may limit ecological validity and clinical adoption.
research_gaps.evidence: Reading Performance in ADHD (2023) used a naturalistic classroom setting but is a single cross-sectional study without replication or protocol validation. No standardized methodology exists for classroom-based eye-tracking in ADHD assessment.
